pois.name: Schweriner Schloss
pois.description: The former residence of the Grand Dukes of Mecklenburg-Schwerin, situated on an island in Lake Schwerin, is one of the most significant works of Romantic Historicism.

pois.name: Doberaner Münster
pois.description: A former Cistercian monastery church known for its exceptional Brick Gothic architecture and a nearly complete ensemble of high-quality medieval interior furnishings.

pois.name: Wismar Altstadt
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site representing the peak of the Hanseatic League, featuring a well-preserved medieval city layout and numerous brick merchant houses.

pois.name: Stralsunder Altstadt
pois.description: Together with Wismar, this UNESCO site showcases the typical Hanseatic town structure with monumental brick churches and the iconic Town Hall.

pois.name: Schloss Güstrow
pois.description: One of the most important Renaissance palaces in Northern Europe, blending Italian and French stylistic elements with Northern German brick architecture.

pois.name: Müritz
pois.description: The largest lake located entirely within Germany, serving as the heart of a national park known for its pristine waters and diverse bird species.

pois.name: Königsstuhl
pois.description: The most famous chalk cliff on Rügen Island, immortalized by Caspar David Friedrich, rising 118 meters above the Baltic Sea.

pois.name: Schloss Ludwigslust
pois.description: A late Baroque palace complex often called the Versailles of Mecklenburg, featuring extensive gardens and a unique interior with 'Ludwigslust cardboard' decorations.

pois.name: Kap Arkona
pois.description: A prominent cape on the Wittow peninsula featuring two historic lighthouses and the remains of the Slavic temple fortress of Jaromarsburg.

pois.name: Kloster Ribnitz
pois.description: A former Poor Clares convent that now houses the German Amber Museum, showcasing the region's long tradition of amber processing.

pois.name: Historisch-Technisches Museum Peenemünde
pois.description: Located in a former power plant, this museum documents the history of the Peenemünde research sites where the first V-2 rockets were developed.

pois.name: Schloss Bothmer
pois.description: The largest Baroque palace complex in the state, built for Count Hans Caspar von Bothmer and famous for its 300-meter-long Festonallee of lime trees.

pois.name: St.-Marien-Kirche Rostock
pois.description: A major Hanseatic brick church known for its astronomical clock from 1472, which is still in working order.

pois.name: Stadtbefestigung Neubrandenburg
pois.description: The best-preserved medieval city wall in Europe, featuring four massive brick gates and numerous small half-timbered houses built into the fortifications.

pois.name: Seebrücke Sellin
pois.description: A reconstructed resort-style pier on Rügen Island, showcasing the typical 'Bäderarchitektur' and extending 394 meters into the Baltic Sea.

pois.name: Schloss Wiligrad
pois.description: A Neo-Renaissance palace built for Duke Johann Albrecht, noted for its terracotta decorations and picturesque location on the steep bank of Lake Schwerin.

pois.name: Greifswalder Dom St. Nikolai
pois.description: The cathedral of Greifswald, a landmark of the city with a distinctive curved spire and a rich collection of medieval and early modern art.

pois.name: Jagdschloss Granitz
pois.description: A popular hunting lodge on Rügen known for its central tower designed by Karl Friedrich Schinkel, offering panoramic views of the island.

pois.name: Großsteingrab Lancken-Granitz
pois.description: A collection of several megalithic passage tombs from the Neolithic era, representing some of the oldest man-made structures in Northern Germany.

pois.name: Karniner Hubbrücke
pois.description: The remains of a massive vertical-lift railway bridge that connected the island of Usedom to the mainland until 1945.

pois.name: Burg Stargard
pois.description: The northernmost hill castle in Germany, once a seat of the Dukes of Mecklenburg-Stargard, featuring extensive medieval fortifications.

pois.name: Klosterruine Eldena
pois.description: The ruins of a Cistercian monastery that became a central motif in the Romantic paintings of Caspar David Friedrich.

pois.name: Warnemünder Leuchtturm
pois.description: An iconic 37-meter-tall lighthouse at the entrance of Rostock harbor, offering views over the Baltic Sea and the 'Teepott' building.

pois.name: Heiligendamm
pois.description: The oldest seaside resort in Germany, famous for its white classical buildings along the promenade, giving it the nickname 'The White City by the Sea'.

pois.name: Schloss Mirow
pois.description: A residence of the Dukes of Mecklenburg-Strelitz located on an island, notable for being the birthplace of Queen Charlotte of Great Britain.

pois.name: Alte Burg Neustadt-Glewe
pois.description: The best-preserved medieval castle in Mecklenburg, featuring a massive keep and original defensive structures.

pois.name: Güstrower Dom
pois.description: A Brick Gothic cathedral housing the famous 'Floating Angel' (Der Schwebende) sculpture by Ernst Barlach.

pois.name: Plauer See
pois.description: The third-largest lake in the Mecklenburg Lake District, popular for water sports and surrounded by historic towns like Plau am See.

pois.name: Prora
pois.description: A massive Nazi-era building complex intended as a Strength Through Joy resort, stretching nearly five kilometers along the coast of Rügen.

pois.name: Schloss Ulrichshusen
pois.description: A reconstructed Renaissance manor house that serves as a major venue for the Mecklenburg-Vorpommern Music Festival.

pois.name: Malchower Kloster
pois.description: A former nunnery located on the shores of Lake Malchow, now housing the Mecklenburg Organ Museum.

pois.name: Burg Klempenow
pois.description: A lowland castle in the Tollense valley that served as a border fortress between Pomerania and Mecklenburg.

pois.name: Schloss Schlemmin
pois.description: A Neo-Gothic manor house built for the von Thun and Hohenstein family, featuring characteristic pointed arches and turrets.

pois.name: Slawenburg Teterow
pois.description: An island in Lake Teterow that was the site of a significant 9th-century Slavic fortress, accessible today via ferry.

pois.name: Gertrudenkapelle Güstrow
pois.description: A small chapel that now serves as a memorial and gallery for the works of sculptor Ernst Barlach, who lived in Güstrow.

pois.name: Alter Strom
pois.description: The historic harbor entrance in Warnemünde, lined with traditional fishing boats, restaurants, and historic captains' houses.

pois.name: Schloss Hohenzieritz
pois.description: A simple yet elegant palace famous as the place where Queen Louise of Prussia died in 1810, now housing a memorial museum.

pois.name: Sternberger See
pois.description: A scenic lake in the Sternberg Lake District Nature Park, known for its clear water and the nearby Slavic castle reconstruction.

pois.name: St. Nikolai Anklam
pois.description: A large Hanseatic brick church that was severely damaged in WWII and now functions as the Otto Lilienthal cultural center.

pois.name: Wasserschloss Quilow
pois.description: A rare example of a Renaissance water castle in Western Pomerania, currently undergoing extensive restoration.

pois.name: Leuchtturm Darßer Ort
pois.description: A red brick lighthouse located at the tip of the Darß peninsula within the Western Pomerania Lagoon Area National Park.

pois.name: Kapelle Vitt
pois.description: An octagonal chapel built for the fishermen of the historic village of Vitt near Kap Arkona, featuring maritime-influenced architecture.

pois.name: Schmaler Luzin
pois.description: A narrow, deep lake in the Feldberg Lake District known for its high water clarity and steep, forested banks.

pois.name: Schloss Penkun
pois.description: A castle with medieval origins that was transformed into a Renaissance residence, located near the Polish border.

pois.name: Kloster Rehna
pois.description: A former Premonstratensian double monastery featuring a well-preserved cloister and a Gothic church.

pois.name: Boizenburg Altstadt
pois.description: A historic town on the Elbe River known for its timber-framed architecture and the 'Fliesenstadt' museum of decorative tiles.

pois.name: Burg Schlitz
pois.description: A grand neoclassical country estate and landscape garden known for its three-winged palace and numerous decorative monuments.

pois.name: Demminer Hanseviertel
pois.description: The historic core of the Hanseatic city of Demmin, located at the confluence of the Peene, Trebel, and Tollense rivers.

pois.name: Schaalsee
pois.description: A deep lake forming the border between Mecklenburg and Schleswig-Holstein, protected as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

pois.name: Schloss Gadebusch
pois.description: A Renaissance palace built with elaborate terracotta reliefs, serving as a rare example of the Johann Albrecht style in brick.

pois.name: Dorfkirche Basedow
pois.description: A village church notable for its richly decorated Renaissance interior and its proximity to the impressive Basedow Castle complex.

pois.name: Schloss Kittendorf
pois.description: A Tudor-style palace designed by Friedrich Hitzig, pupil of Schinkel, situated in an extensive English landscape park.

pois.name: Alte Burg Penzlin
pois.description: Known as the 'Witches' Castle', it features a museum dedicated to the history of witch trials in the region.

pois.name: Schloss Spyker
pois.description: A striking red Renaissance palace on Rügen that once belonged to the Swedish Field Marshal Carl Gustaf Wrangel.

pois.name: Putbus Circus
pois.description: A perfectly circular plaza surrounded by white Neoclassical buildings, part of the planned 'Residenzstadt' founded by Prince Wilhelm Malte I.

pois.name: Teufelsstein bei Lubmin
pois.description: One of the largest glacial erratics in the state, located just off the coast in the Baltic Sea near Lubmin.

pois.name: Störtebeker Braumanufaktur
pois.description: A historic brewery in Stralsund established in 1827, continuing the city's long tradition of Hanseatic beer production.

pois.name: Tollensesee
pois.description: A large, clean lake adjacent to Neubrandenburg, popular for recreation and known for its scenic hilly surroundings.

pois.name: St. Petri Wolgast
pois.description: A Gothic church that houses the ducal vault of the Pomeranian dukes and features a historic cycle of Danse Macabre paintings.

pois.name: Alter Schwede Wismar
pois.description: The oldest secular building in Wismar, built in 1380, featuring a magnificent brick gabled facade and serving as a traditional inn.
